text.title: rag-architecture Failure Modes
text.summary: Three observable antipatterns when designing RAG architecture.

antipattern.description: Building a RAG system without a written architecture decision record.
antipattern.reason: Future maintainers re-litigate every decision. Detector: no markdown decision record in repo. Fix: enforce r1-two-phase-pipeline output as the deliverable for this methodology.

antipattern.description: Choosing a single top_k value for all query complexities.
antipattern.reason: Simple queries waste context budget; multi-hop queries lack coverage. Detector: faithfulness varies wildly across query types. Fix: route by query type — agentic-RAG for multi-hop (decision-tree branch).

antipattern.description: Decision record omits numeric quality gates.
antipattern.reason: Without thresholds, "good enough" is subjective and regressions slide. Detector: metrics block missing or 0. Fix: enforce r5-eval-before-launch — require MRR / faithfulness / latency / cost numeric thresholds.
